perturbation.original_text: 1.4 "Material" means the source code of the Package comprising the latest technical information and documentation described in Schedules 1 and 2;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 "Material" means the object code of the Package comprising the latest technical information and documentation described in Schedules 1 and 2;
perturbation.explanation: The term "Material" is initially defined as the "source code." This perturbation changes the definition to "object code." This introduces an internal contradiction because the agreement relies on the "Material" being the source code for verification, maintenance, and release purposes, which would not be possible if it were object code.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 The Owner warrants that: 2.2.1 it owns the Intellectual Property Rights in the Material and has authority to enter into this Agreement; and 2.2.2 the Material lodged under Clause 2.1 shall contain all information in human-readable form and on suitable media to enable a reasonably skilled programmer or analyst to understand, maintain and correct the Package without the assistance of any other person.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Material' as 'source code', which Clause 2.2.2 relies on by stating that the 'Material' should contain 'all information in human-readable form' to enable maintenance. The changed text redefines 'Material' as 'object code', which is not human-readable and thus directly contradicts Clause 2.2.2. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because the altered definition prevents the fulfillment of the requirements in Clause 2.2.2, leading to an inconsistency in obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Subject to the provisions of Clauses 6.2 and 6.3, NCC shall release the Material to a duly authorised officer of the Licensee if at any time or times any of the following events or circumstances occur, arise or become apparent:
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Subject to the provisions of Clauses 6.2 and 6.3, NCC shall release the Product to a duly authorised officer of the Licensee if at any time or times any of the following events or circumstances occur, arise or become apparent:
perturbation.explanation: The term "Material" which was described earlier is now replace by "Product". "Product" is never defined and the usage of Product and Material interchangeably creates confusion around the obligation to provide the correct item when releasing under certain conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Subject to the provisions of Clauses 6.2 and 6.3, NCC shall release the Product to a duly authorised officer of the Licensee if at any time or times any of the following events or circumstances occur, arise or become apparent: 6.1.1 the Owner enters into any composition or arrangement with its creditors or (being a company) enters into liquidation whether compulsory or voluntary (other than for the purposes of solvent reconstruction or amalgamation) or has a receiver or administrative receiver appointed over all or any part of its assets or undertaking or a petition is presented for an Administration Order or (being an individual or partnership) becomes bankrupt, or an event occurs within the jurisdiction of the country in which the Owner is situated which has a similar effect to any of the above events in the United Kingdom; or 6.1.2 the Owner ceases to trade; or 6.1.3 the Owner assigns copyright in the Material and the assignee fails within 60 days of such assignment to offer the Licensee substantially similar protection to that provided by this Agreement without significantly increasing the cost to the Licensee; or 6.1.4 the Owner without legal justification, has defaulted to a material degree in any obligation to provide maintenance or modification of the Package under the Licence Agreement or any maintenance agreement entered into in connection with the Package and has failed to remedy such default notified by the Licensee to the Owner; or 6.1.5 coding of the Package is such that either the accuracy or the functionality or the performance of the Package is or becomes or is demonstrably likely to become significantly adversely affected by the entry or processing of data incorporating any date or dates whether prior or subsequent to or including 31 December 1999, including but not limited to any of the following: 6.1.5.1 the Package crashes at any time while processing any such data; 6.1.5.2. the Owner has warranted or represented that the Package is capable of accurately and correctly processing such data in accordance with the Package's current functional specification and the Licensee demonstrates that the Package is not so capable; 6.1.5.3 the Owner has undertaken or attempted to procure the Package to be so capable and the Licensee demonstrates that the Package is still not so capable; 6.1.5.4 no such warranty, representation, undertaking or attempt has been given or made and the Licensee demonstrates that the Package is not so capable.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Material" to "Product" introduces a contradiction. The original contract likely defines "Material" and its associated obligations. The introduction of "Product" without definition, especially within the same clause as the contradicted text, creates ambiguity. The contradicted text refers to "Product," but if "Product" is not defined anywhere and "Material" used to be defined, it makes the obligation unclear. Which should be released? The change creates ambiguity around which object is being referred to and how the obligations should be interpreted. This falls under the definition of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction", as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations regarding which item should be released under specific conditions.

perturbation.original_text: 9.2 Upon the Material being lodged with NCC, NCC shall perform those tests in accordance with its Standard Verification Service and shall provide a copy of the test report to the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.2 Upon the Artifact being lodged with NCC, NCC shall perform those tests in accordance with its Standard Verification Service and shall provide a copy of the test report to the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Material” with "Artifact,” a term not previously defined in the contract. The original text defines "Material" clearly in section 1.4, thus altering this to "Artifact” brings up the question of what actually are the tests being performed upon, and are they the tests reffering to the material. This leads to uncertainty in enforcement because the tests required by the standard verification service will likely depend on what is lodged with NCC, and if “Artifact” is undefined, the nature and scope of the verification become ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.2 Upon the Artifact being lodged with NCC, NCC shall perform those tests in accordance with its Standard Verification Service and shall provide a copy of the test report to the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'Material' with 'Artifact', an undefined term. Section 9.2, which appears in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text, specifies tests to be performed based on what is lodged with NCC. Because 'Artifact' lacks a clear definition, it directly creates ambiguity and uncertainty about the nature of the tests, thus contradicting the intent of the clause. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, as the contract uses a term without defining it, leading to conflicting interpretations of the obligations.
